title: Automated Table Addition Within Excel Reports
excerpt: When generating Excel Reports, could auto-sizing of columns take place as well as auto-tabling of the rows/columns as branded rows with the option to deselect branded rows so that data is simpler to review? Also, could c…

posts.author: andrewst (Tyler Andrews)
posts.cookedHtml: When generating Excel Reports, could auto-sizing of columns take place as well as auto-tabling of the rows/columns as branded rows with the option to deselect branded rows so that data is simpler to review? Also, could columns and any column summaries that contain “$” automatically be setup as a currency value with “$” signs in the reports?

posts.author: peta (Peta Bolger)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @andrewst

Short answer is no. Right now, the export from monday.com is as it comes, and data will need to be formatted in excel if the native output won’t do. It’s a good suggestion though - I recommend you add this on as a feature request in the Community.

You can do that via the ‘+ Start a discussion’ button - just be sure to associate with the Feature Requests category.

Could you set up a dashboard to display the data in a palatable way and then export that dashboard view to pdf? That might help you from another angle.

Best of luck!

Peta | Upstream Tech
peta@upstreamtech.io

posts.author: andrewst (Tyler Andrews)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @peta

Thank you kindly, I “+ Start(ed) a discussion” with association to the Feature Requests category in a new posting. I am new to posting on the monday.com community site and I am unsure how to look up the name of the new post I just made (it’s slightly different than how this one is named). Once a reply is received on the new post and I receive a notification email, I will visit the post and share the posting name on this post so yourself and others may visit!

I need to learn Dashboards next and see how I could build one to compare to how an Excel report export looks. Thanks for your suggestion, I may be able to take the info we have in our board and make a sweet-looking dashboard out of it that still shows Formula column summaries and other data in an intuitive manner.

posts.author: andrewst (Tyler Andrews)
posts.cookedHtml:
Found the new post name! Once it is approved, it will be called:

“Excel Reports - Automated Table Addition, Column Sizing, and Currency Value On Columns That Contain “$” Within The Board”
